title: GPT-5.5 is a total freak
summary: The AI Search channel presents a review of OpenAI's GPT-5.5, claiming it is exceptional. The video demonstrates the model's capabilities across diverse tasks including code generation, 3D rendering, cancer detection, music composition, and deep research, while also addressing hallucination issues and providing performance benchmarks.
writeup: A hands-on stress test of GPT-5.5 across complex coding tasks and medical imaging, using OpenAI's Codex app for agentic multi-file project work. The video pushes the model on tasks that simpler benchmarks skip: interactive 3D visualization, physically-based rendering, and radiological interpretation. The through-line is a realistic capability profile, showing where the model's coding prowess is genuinely impressive and where it falls apart under expert-level medical demands.
key_points: GPT-5.5 is a thinking model available in ChatGPT and in the Codex app; the Codex app is recommended for complex builds because it supports multiple parallel agents working across an entire project folder, with iterative refinement across turns.

A fully interactive 3D Earth digital twin with zoom from orbit to street-level, night lights, and rendered 3D buildings was produced in three prompts inside Codex, demonstrating that the model can decompose and iterate on a visually complex specification without scaffolding.

A ray-tracing simulation with three objects (sphere, cube, pyramid), per-object sliders for position, reflectivity, roughness, translucency, IOR, and color, and correct cross-object reflections was completed in three prompts and delivered as a standalone HTML file that ran in the browser.

On chest CT lesion detection GPT-5.5 correctly identified and circled 3 of 4 lesions; on a harder six-image brain tumor classification task it scored 0 of 6 correct, misidentifying tumors as aneurysms, arteriovenous malformations, and other non-tumor findings, showing the model is not reliable for diagnostic radiology.
The Codex interface exposes a thinking effort setting; selecting extended effort increases reasoning depth and is the configuration used for the most demanding tasks in the video.
